title: Programming Foundations
summary: An absolute-beginner bridge from everyday computer use to reading, tracing, designing, running, testing and explaining small programs.

assumed_knowledge: Ability to use a keyboard and web browser
audience.target_capability: Independently express a small problem as an algorithm, trace program state, use basic developer tools, and enter a language course with reliable mental models.

includes: Responsible learning and use of assistance
non_goals: Fluency in a specific programming language
Advanced computer architecture or operating systems

authoritative_sources.title: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct
Organizing Instruction and Study to Improve Student Learning

runtime.execution_environment: Browser simulations and language-neutral tracing exercises
notes: Concrete syntax appears only as labeled comparison examples.
Accessibility-safe visual traces must also expose textual state descriptions.

modules.title: Programs and computers
modules.description: Build an accurate first mental model of software and execution.

skills.title: Program, code and algorithm
skills.description: Distinguish a program, its source code, and the algorithm it implements.
outcomes: Classify examples as source code, programs, algorithms, inputs or outputs.

skills.title: Hardware and software roles
skills.description: Relate processor, memory, storage, devices, operating system and application software.
outcomes: Explain where instructions and data reside while a small program runs.

skills.title: Source-to-execution models
skills.description: Recognize compilation, interpretation and virtual-machine execution without false absolutes.
outcomes: Trace a high-level source file through a named toolchain to observable output.

skills.title: Instructions, state and determinism
skills.description: Understand that execution follows defined instructions over changing state, subject to inputs and environment.
outcomes: Explain why two runs may agree or differ using input, state and environment.

modules.title: Computational problem solving
modules.description: Turn an informal need into precise inputs, outputs and steps.

skills.title: Inputs, outputs and constraints
skills.description: State what a problem receives, produces and must respect.
outcomes: Write an unambiguous input-output-constraint statement for a small problem.

skills.title: Decomposition
skills.description: Split a problem into cohesive, ordered subproblems.
outcomes: Create and justify a decomposition whose parts cover the original requirement.

skills.title: Pseudocode
skills.description: Express executable intent without depending on one language's syntax.
outcomes: Write and trace structured pseudocode for a calculation and decision.

skills.title: Examples and edge cases
skills.description: Use representative, boundary and invalid cases to refine a problem statement.
outcomes: Produce normal, boundary and invalid examples with expected results.

modules.title: Values, state and expressions
modules.description: Reason about data and state changes before learning concrete syntax.

skills.title: Values and types
skills.description: Distinguish numbers, truth values, text and collections by valid operations.
outcomes: Choose a suitable conceptual type and reject invalid operations for a value.

skills.title: Variables and assignment
skills.description: Model a variable as a named part of program state whose value may change.
outcomes: Trace variable values through a sequence of assignments without treating assignment as algebraic equality.

skills.title: Expressions and evaluation
skills.description: Evaluate operations using operands, precedence and intermediate results.
outcomes: Evaluate mixed arithmetic, comparison and Boolean expressions step by step.

skills.title: Mutation and aliases
skills.description: Recognize the conceptual difference between rebinding a name and changing a shared value.
outcomes: Predict visible changes when two names refer to the same mutable collection.

modules.title: Control flow
modules.description: Trace and design sequential, selective and repetitive execution.

skills.title: Sequence
skills.description: Trace ordered statements and identify dependencies between them.
outcomes: Produce a correct state table for sequential pseudocode.

skills.title: Selection
skills.description: Use Boolean conditions to choose among mutually appropriate paths.
outcomes: Design and trace nested and multi-branch decisions including boundary cases.

skills.title: Iteration
skills.description: Use counted and condition-controlled repetition with correct progress.
outcomes: Design and trace loops while identifying initialization, condition, update and termination.

skills.title: Nested control and tables
skills.description: Reason about decisions and loops nested inside one another, including grid traversal.
outcomes: Trace nested loops over a small two-dimensional table and count executions.

modules.title: Functions and abstraction
modules.description: Organize repeated logic behind named, testable interfaces.

skills.title: Functions as contracts
skills.description: Describe a function by purpose, inputs, outputs and observable effects.
outcomes: Specify a small function contract independently of its implementation.

skills.title: Calls, parameters and returns
skills.description: Trace argument binding, local execution and returned results.
outcomes: Trace nested function calls using separate call frames.

skills.title: Local scope
skills.description: Distinguish local names from state visible outside a function.
outcomes: Predict name visibility and avoid accidental dependence on global state.

skills.title: Recursion concept
skills.description: Understand base cases and smaller self-similar calls without requiring language syntax.
outcomes: Trace a simple recursive definition and identify a missing or unreachable base case.

modules.title: Structured data
modules.description: Select and operate on common conceptual data organizations.

skills.title: Sequences and indexes
skills.description: Use ordered collections and valid positions, including empty and boundary cases.
outcomes: Trace indexed reads, updates and traversal without off-by-one errors.

skills.title: Records and entities
skills.description: Group related named fields that describe one conceptual entity.
outcomes: Design a record representation and justify its fields and types.

skills.title: Sets and maps
skills.description: Distinguish unique membership from key-to-value lookup.
outcomes: Choose between sequence, set and map for a stated access requirement.

skills.title: Data-structure choice
skills.description: Compare data structures using order, duplicates, lookup, update and traversal needs.
outcomes: Defend a data-structure choice and identify a reasonable alternative's trade-off.

skills.title: Files, folders and paths
skills.description: Create, locate and organize source and data files using paths and extensions.
outcomes: Construct and explain relative and absolute paths in a small project tree.

skills.title: Editor and terminal workflow
skills.description: Edit, save, navigate and run a supplied command without confusing source and output.
outcomes: Use a terminal simulator to navigate a project and run a named tool.

skills.title: Error categories and messages
skills.description: Differentiate syntax/build, runtime and logic failures and extract actionable location information.
outcomes: Classify an error, identify the earliest useful diagnostic and propose the next observation.

skills.title: Debugging and testing loop
skills.description: Form a hypothesis, isolate a case, inspect state, change one cause and rerun tests.
outcomes: Repair a flawed pseudocode routine and provide tests that expose the original defect.

modules.title: Responsible and durable practice
modules.description: Use feedback, documentation and assistance without outsourcing understanding.

skills.title: Reading before writing
skills.description: Trace, annotate and explain existing code-like procedures before modifying them.
outcomes: Explain a supplied routine's purpose, state changes and edge cases.

skills.title: Using feedback
skills.description: Convert correctness feedback into a diagnosis, correction and new test.
outcomes: Explain why an answer failed and demonstrate a corrected transfer attempt.

skills.title: Documentation and AI assistance
skills.description: Ask bounded questions, verify claims and retain ownership of reasoning and submitted work.
outcomes: Evaluate an assisted answer against a source, tests and the stated problem.

skills.title: Integrity and attribution
skills.description: Distinguish collaboration, reference use, attribution and plagiarism in programming work.
outcomes: Choose and justify an ethical action in realistic learning and reuse scenarios.

exit_outcomes: Translate a small informal problem into inputs, outputs, constraints, examples and structured pseudocode.
Trace values, control flow, function calls and structured data while explaining the resulting output.
Use a basic editor-terminal workflow and classify, investigate and repair common program failures.
Design meaningful tests and use documentation or AI assistance without surrendering verification or authorship.
